questions.question_text: The table shows the length (in cm) of 45 pieces of wood in a factory.\n\n$$\begin{array}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline \text{Length} & 30-34 & 35-39 & 40-44 & 45-49 & 50-54 & 55-59 & 60-64 & 65-69 & 70-74 \\\hline \text{Number of pieces of wood} & 2 & 2 & q - 1 & 4 & 3q - 5 & q^2 - 3 & 10 & 3 & 1 \\\hline \end{array}$$

sub_questions.question_text: (b) Using the value of $q$ in 13 (a), draw a cumulative frequency curve for the distribution.
(c) Using the curve in 13 (b), find the:

questions.question_text: The vertices of a quadrilateral are $K(-1, 3)$, $L(2, 5)$, $Q(6, -3)$ and $R(m - 1, n - 2)$. If $\overline{KL}$ is equal to $\overline{RQ}$, find:

sub_questions.question_text: (b) angle $LKR$, correct to the nearest degree.

questions.question_text:
(a) An object of mass $20 \ kg$ is suspended at a point $G$ by two light inextensible strings $XG$ and $YG$. The strings are inclined at $55^{\circ}$ and $35^{\circ}$ respectively to the downward vertical. Find, correct to four significant figures, the tensions in the strings.

[Take $g = 10 \ ms^{-2}$]

sub_questions.question_text: (b) A bullet of mass $180 \ g$ is fired horizontally into a fixed wooden block with a speed of $24 \ ms^{-1}$. If the bullet is brought to rest in $0.4 \ sec$ by a constant resistance, calculate the distance moved by the bullet in the wood.
